paper_title: Material and methods of Hurleg Lake study

methods.description: Collection of water samples from various sources around the Hurleg Lake region to study isotopic variability.

steps.description: A total of 56 water samples were collected from rain, lakes, rivers, springs/wells, and puddles around the Hurleg Lake region.
steps.searchable_summary: collect water samples from various sources around Hurleg Lake

methods.description: Collection and dating of sediment core HL06-1 to establish a chronological framework for isotopic analysis.

steps.description: Core HL06-1 was obtained in July 2006 at 7.6 m water depth using a UWITEC corer.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment core HL06-1 using UWITEC corer

steps.description: The age model was established through AMS-14 C dates on the aquatic plant Ruppia from selected intervals.
steps.searchable_summary: establish age model using AMS-14 C dating on Ruppia

methods.description: Preparation and analysis of sediment samples for n-FAs and alkenones, including compound specific δD analysis.

steps.description: The total lipids were extracted from the freeze-dried sediments with organic solvents (dichloromethane:methanol = 9:1, v/v).
steps.searchable_summary: extract total lipids from freeze-dried sediments using organic solvents

steps.description: After being hydrolyzed by 6% potassium hydroxide in methanol solution, the neutral fraction was extracted with n-hexane.
steps.searchable_summary: hydrolyze and extract neutral fraction with n-hexane

steps.description: The basic solution was acidified to pH < 1 to extract the carboxylic acid fraction with n-hexane.
steps.searchable_summary: acidify solution and extract carboxylic acid fraction with n-hexane

steps.description: The neutral fraction was then separated into three fractions with silica gel column chromatography using eluents of n-hexane, dichloromethane and methanol.
steps.searchable_summary: separate neutral fraction using silica gel column chromatography

steps.description: The alkenone and n-FA fractions were analyzed on Gas Chromatography equipped with a flame ionization detector.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze alkenone and n-FA fractions using Gas Chromatography

methods.description: Analysis of water and compound specific hydrogen isotopes using isotope ratio mass spectrometry.

steps.description: The water oxygen and hydrogen isotopes were analyzed by isotope ratio mass spectrometry (IRMS) at the University of Arizona Isotope Lab.
Compound specific hydrogen values of n-FAs were measured by the TRACE GC Ultra-Delta V IRMS.
steps.searchable_summary: measure compound specific hydrogen values using TRACE GC Ultra-Delta V IRMS
